items.prompt: Compute `3/4 + 1/8` by rewriting to common units and show the equivalent fractions you used.
items.expected_evidence: explain why 5/4 represents one whole and one fourth more
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates explain why 5/4 represents one whole and one fourth more with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Compute `5/6 - 1/4` and justify the common denominator choice.
items.expected_evidence: match bounded mixed numbers and improper fractions that describe the same quantity
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates match bounded mixed numbers and improper fractions that describe the same quantity with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Place `2/3` and `5/8` on the same number line and determine which is greater.
items.expected_evidence: compare simple magnitudes greater than one whole without reverting to whole-number-only logic
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates compare simple magnitudes greater than one whole without reverting to whole-number-only logic with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain why `3/4` can be rewritten as `6/8` without changing its value.
items.expected_evidence: explain why 5/4 represents one whole and one fourth more
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to explain why 5/4 represents one whole and one fourth more.

items.prompt: Explain why fractions can only be combined directly when they refer to equal-sized parts.
items.expected_evidence: match bounded mixed numbers and improper fractions that describe the same quantity
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to match bounded mixed numbers and improper fractions that describe the same quantity.

items.prompt: A student says `3/4 + 1/8 = 4/12`. Diagnose the first structural mistake. Focus on whole number override.
items.expected_evidence: explain why 5/4 represents one whole and one fourth more
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward explain why 5/4 represents one whole and one fourth more.

items.prompt: A student rewrites `2/3` as `3/4` before adding. Identify the equivalence error. Focus on mixed improper disconnect.
items.expected_evidence: match bounded mixed numbers and improper fractions that describe the same quantity
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward match bounded mixed numbers and improper fractions that describe the same quantity.

items.prompt: A recipe uses `3/4` cup of oats and `1/8` cup of seeds. How much is used altogether?
items.expected_evidence: explain why 5/4 represents one whole and one fourth more
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves explain why 5/4 represents one whole and one fourth more in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: A hiker walks `5/6` mile in the morning and `1/4` mile less in the afternoon. How much farther was the morning walk?
items.expected_evidence: match bounded mixed numbers and improper fractions that describe the same quantity
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves match bounded mixed numbers and improper fractions that describe the same quantity in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: After a delay, solve `2/3 + 1/6` and explain why the rewrite keeps the quantity unchanged.
items.expected_evidence: explain why 5/4 represents one whole and one fourth more
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates explain why 5/4 represents one whole and one fourth more on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Before closing fraction magnitude beyond one whole, complete a prerequisite check on compare fractions and explain how that prior understanding supports the fraction reasoning.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether compare fractions is secure enough to support fraction magnitude beyond one whole
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in compare fractions from a current-node issue in fraction magnitude beyond one whole.
